question: [File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user send a high volume of concurrent GET /api/v1/balances requests with no filters, each triggering a correlated getTokenAccountBalanceSubQuery() subquery per returned row, exhausting the DB connection pool (max: config.db.pool.maxConnections) and causing 30%+ of mirror node REST instances to become unresponsive to all other traffic?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request with a timestamp parameter (e.g., timestamp=lte:9999999999.999999999) that causes getBalances() to fire two sequential pool.queryQuietly() calls — one in getTsQuery() and one for the main query — holding two DB connections per request, and by sending many such requests simultaneously, exhaust the connection pool and degrade 30%+ of processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getAccountBalanceTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp=lte:<very_large_value> parameter that causes getAccountBalanceTimestampRange() to scan a wide range of the account_balance table partition, resulting in a long-running query that holds a DB connection open and, when repeated concurrently, starves the pool and degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getOptimizedTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged external user supply the maximum allowed number of timestamp=ne:<value> parameters (up to maxRepeatedQueryParameters), causing getOptimizedTimestampRange() to accumulate a large neParams array and getAccountBalanceTimestampRange() to append a not consensus_timestamp = any($4) clause with a large array, forcing a full partition scan and causing sustained high CPU/IO on the DB server that degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getTokenBalanceSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a timestamp filter, causing getBalancesQuery() to embed a correlated getTokenBalanceSubQuery() with json_agg(json_build_object(...)) for every account row returned, and by requesting the maximum limit (100 accounts × 50 token balances each), generate a query that performs 100 correlated subqueries with json aggregation, causing DB CPU exhaustion and degrading 30%+ of nodes when sent concurrently?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getTokenAccountBalanceSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with no timestamp filter, causing getBalances() to use the entity_balance CTE path with getTokenAccountBalanceSubQuery() as a correlated subquery for each of the 100 returned rows, and by sending many such requests concurrently, cause sustained DB load that degrades 30%+ of mirror node processing nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getAccountIdsByPublicKey()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an account.publickey parameter in a request with a timestamp filter, causing getBalances() to fire a third sequential pool.queryQuietly() call in getAccountIdsByPublicKey() (in addition to getTsQuery() and the main query), holding three DB connections per request, and by sending many such requests concurrently, exhaust the connection pool and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests combining all accepted parameters (account.id, account.balance, account.publickey, timestamp, limit, order) simultaneously, causing getBalances() to execute the maximum number of sequential async DB calls (getTsQuery, getAccountIdsByPublicKey, getBalancesQuery with nested token subquery), and by sending many such maximally-complex requests concurrently, degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getOptimizedTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp=gte:1 combined with timestamp=lte:9999999999999999999 to force getOptimizedTimestampRange() to compute a near-maximum BigInt range, causing getAccountBalanceTimestampRange() to scan across multiple monthly partitions of the account_balance table, resulting in a full-table-equivalent scan that causes DB I/O exhaustion and degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: parseAccountIdQueryParam()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an EVM address as account.id (e.g., account.id=0xabcdef...40chars), causing parseAccountIdQueryParam() to call EntityService.getEncodedId() which fires an additional async DB lookup, and by sending many such requests concurrently, add extra DB queries per request that exhaust the connection pool and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: parseAccountIdQueryParam()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an account alias (e.g., account.id=AAAQEAYEAUDAOCAJCAIREEYUCULBOGAZ) as account.id, causing parseAccountIdQueryParam() to call EntityService.getAccountIdFromAlias() which fires an additional async DB lookup, and by sending many such requests concurrently, add extra DB queries per request that exhaust the connection pool and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBalancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a timestamp filter that results in getBalancesQuery() constructing a query with SELECT DISTINCT ON (account_id) over the account_balance table combined with a correlated token_balance subquery using DISTINCT ON (token_id), causing the DB to perform two nested distinct operations and json_agg aggregation, and by sending many such requests concurrently, cause DB CPU exhaustion that degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getBalances() awaits accountParamsPromise (Promise.all) before proceeding, and by supplying an EVM address account.id that triggers EntityService.getEncodedId() concurrently with many requests, cause a thundering herd of DB lookups that exhausts the connection pool and degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getAccountBalanceTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp=eq:<value_near_epoch_start> parameter that causes getOptimizedTimestampRange() to set lowerBound to the first day of a very early month, forcing getAccountBalanceTimestampRange() to scan a large historical partition of account_balance, resulting in a slow query that holds a DB connection and degrades 30%+ of nodes when sent concurrently?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getTokenBalanceSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a timestamp filter and limit=100, causing getBalancesQuery() to embed a getTokenBalanceSubQuery() that performs json_agg over up to 50 token_balance rows per account, resulting in a query that aggregates up to 5000 token balance rows per request, and by sending many such requests concurrently, cause DB memory pressure that degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with account.balance=gte:0 (matching all accounts) combined with limit=100 and a timestamp filter, causing getBalances() to return the maximum result set with full token balance aggregation, and by sending many such requests concurrently, cause sustained high memory and CPU usage on the Node.js process that degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: formatBalancesResult()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a response with 100 balance rows each containing 50 token balances, causing formatBalancesResult() to call utils.parseTokenBalances() 100 times and EntityId.parse() up to 5100 times per request, and by sending many such requests concurrently, cause CPU exhaustion in the Node.js event loop that degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getOptimizedTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged external user supply multiple timestamp parameters (e.g., timestamp=gte:X&timestamp=lte:Y&timestamp=ne:Z1&timestamp=ne:Z2...) up to maxRepeatedQueryParameters, causing getOptimizedTimestampRange() to iterate over all of them with BigInt() conversions and comparisons, and by sending many such requests concurrently, cause CPU exhaustion in the Node.js process that degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getAccountIdsByPublicKey()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a valid public key that matches many accounts in the entity table, causing getAccountIdsByPublicKey() to return a large ids array and construct a large ab.account_id in (...) SQL fragment, and when this is embedded in the main query, cause a large IN clause that forces a full index scan and degrades 30%+ of nodes when sent concurrently?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the two-query sequential pattern in getBalances() (getTsQuery() then main query) to hold a DB connection for the duration of both queries, and by sending requests at a rate just below the rate-limit threshold but high enough to keep all pool connections occupied, cause a sustained connection pool exhaustion that degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getTokenAccountBalanceSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with no timestamp filter and account.balance=gte:0, causing getBalances() to execute a CTE query with a correlated getTokenAccountBalanceSubQuery() for each of 100 returned rows, where each subquery scans token_account with ta.associated=true, and by sending many such requests concurrently, cause DB I/O exhaustion that degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBalancesQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a timestamp filter that causes getBalancesQuery() to use tsParams = [upper, lower, upper, lower, upper] (5 timestamp parameters), combined with accountParams, balanceParams, and limit params via mergeParams(), creating a large parameter array that increases query planning overhead, and by sending many such requests concurrently, degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with a timestamp filter set to a value where no balance snapshot exists (e.g., a future timestamp), causing getTsQuery() to return an empty object and getBalances() to return early, but still consuming one DB connection per request for the getAccountBalanceTimestampRange() lookup, and by sending many such requests concurrently, exhaust the DB connection pool and degrade 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getAccountBalanceTimestampRange()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a timestamp=lte:<current_time_ns> parameter that causes getAccountBalanceTimestampRange() to query the account_balance table with a range spanning the current month and the previous month (due to getOptimizedTimestampRange() setting lowerBound to the first day of the previous month), forcing a two-partition scan, and by sending many such requests concurrently, cause DB I/O exhaustion that degrades 30%+ of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rest/balances.js -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getTokenBalanceSubQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a timestamp filter and order=asc, causing getTokenBalanceSubQuery() to use order=asc in the DISTINCT ON subquery (order by token_id asc, consensus_timestamp desc), and by sending many such requests concurrently with alternating order=asc and order=desc to prevent query plan caching, cause increased DB query planning overhead that degrades 30%+ of nodes?
